mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy, which lowers discount rates and boosts equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS is declining, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates signal weakening labor market conditions, which may lead to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances despite rising UNRATE, or t-test p-value > 0.05 for return differences.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising federal funds rates and falling inflation (CPI) signals a tightening monetary policy with disinflation, which may lead to positive S&P 500 returns as investors anticipate stable growth.
failure_condition: The joint effect of FEDFUNDS increasing and CPIAUCSL decreasing does not result in positive 20d returns, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During supply-side disruptions, declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals reduced economic output, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances despite declining INDPRO, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, which boosts economic activity and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances when HOUST is rising, or t-test p-value > 0.05 for return differences.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) reflects a robust labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances despite PAYEMS growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which drives economic growth and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances despite rising INDPRO, or t-test p-value > 0.05 for return differences.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising GDP (GDPC1) and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals strong economic growth without inflationary pressures, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 increasing and CPIAUCSL stable does not result in positive 60d returns, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
